poem.content:
The subway rumbles, a bleak heart of iron,
Another region swallowed by the grey.
A pilot’s stare, unblinking, thereon,
Across the tracks, where shadows sway.

He grips his pen, to sketch a life untold,
A loyal dog asleep upon my stool.
Another armed figure, hard and cold,
Within this urban, broken rule.

The lumber hauled, a hood obscures his face,
A buyer waits, with empty, hollow eyes.
No outdoor light to grace this dismal place,
Just fleeting dreams behind a false disguise.

He dreams of ranch, of open, sunlit space,
A sculpted model, posed in morning dew.
But reality brings only this disgrace,
A canvas stained with shades of somber hue.

He tries to bring the loyal feeling back,
Lost in the urban sprawl, and endless chase,
A soul adrift, upon a barren track,
Leaving only the bleak feel of this place.
